meaning: adj.无显著特点的,不能适应特殊环境的
examples: She experienced an increase in generalized aches and pains.
These conclusions cannot be generalized to the whole country.
Mood may be defined as a temporary and mild positive or negative feeling that is generalized and not tied to any particular circumstance.
On the other hand, making these kinds of generalized assertions and arguing limited cases proves nothing.
Petitioner failed to provide a business plan that included more than just generalized goals.
